# Transcrição: 01-Social Media 101

- **Fonte:** 01-Social Media 101
- **Duração:** 06:14
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

In today's digital age, social media has become a vital tool for businesses of all sizes. It's not just a platform for socializing and entertainment, but a powerful medium for marketing, networking, and building a strong brand presence. Here are five ways that social media can benefit your business. First, it's reaching your customers. Social media platforms have billions of active users worldwide, meaning you have the perfect opportunity to reach those ideal clients no matter where they are in the world. Social media also allows you to engage directly with your audience. You can answer questions, get feedback, and build relationships in real time. Think of it like a first handshake with your audience. With consistent posting and engagement, you can also increase brand awareness and visibility, making it easier for potential customers to find and connect with you. Compared to traditional marketing methods, social media marketing is cost effective. Even with a small budget, you can reach a large audience and see significant results. And lastly, social media platforms provide valuable insight about your audience's behavior, preferences, and demographics. This data can guide your marketing strategies and help you make informed decisions. Remember, social media is not just about selling. It's about being where your customers are and connecting with them on a personal level, all while you're creating valuable content as your authentic self. With so many users and so many platforms, how do you pick the right one for your business? Let me give you a brief overview of some of the biggest platforms people are using right now. Let's start with Facebook. You're probably pretty familiar with Facebook as a personal user. So you know it's a great way to engage with friends and family and brands that you like. However, Facebook is also known for its groups and communities, bringing like-minded people together around a common hobby, topic, or lifestyle. Facebook groups are a goldmine for finding the people who will respond to your coaching or your product because they go into these communities looking for advice. Some other great platforms are TikTok, for instance. It's really starting to gain some traction with younger users because it has a focus on short, engaging, super-sharable videos. Instagram, on the other hand, is still all about visuals. The platform started with photos and now has evolved as a rival to TikTok in these videos called Reels. You've probably started seeing the power of Reels and people even re-sharing content on their Instagram from TikTok. And lastly is LinkedIn. This is ideal for business professionals who are targeting other businesses and really seeking to have more of the corporate conversations.

## [03:00 - 06:02]

This can be ideal for the executive coaches and people who really enjoy being in the corporate space. Think of LinkedIn as your online resume. So coming into these different spaces, you really might be asking yourself, where do you start? The answer is wherever your ideal client spends the most time. Do your research and build your presence on the platforms that your audience is already using. Here's an example. If you're a fitness and a wellness coach, Instagram is a great way to document your daily life and the transformation that you've had and really drawing in people using the pictures as well as the Reels. If you're a course creator and maybe you do something like dog training, for instance, you're going to want to find other dog lovers on Facebook using Facebook groups so you can interact and they're looking for advice from people just like you. If you're just starting out, focus on building your presence on one platform at a time instead of trying to spread yourself thin on every single platform. Quality really matters here. If you already have a following on when platform and want to expand, take this as your opportunity to start a second account or a third on a new platform. Think about where you might find a new segment for your audience. For example, say you're the wellness coach from earlier and you're thriving on Instagram. You may want to consider TikTok or even YouTube to share your tutorial video content to different audiences or you can go into Facebook groups and start getting into transformation communities and sharing your stories so you stand out as an authority in those spaces. When do you know you're ready to move to a second platform though? When you start feeling that connectivity is getting really strong in one place and you're really realizing that you're creating Reels on Instagram and they're getting a high influx and maybe you're catching yourself doing tutorials and they're coming up in parts. Maybe it's time for you to use Longform video on YouTube, for instance. Now you can create a YouTube account where people can subscribe to this content and you can encourage your TikTok viewers or your Instagram viewers to go and follow you on YouTube. When we start looking at all of these different platforms, it's important that you choose the platform that you feel most encouraged to post on. Sometimes we start feeling resistance when we start playing with the different platforms. Again, you do not want to spread yourself too thin. You do not need to be on all five platforms to be successful. That's why I want to genuinely encourage you to start with one, maximize that one, learn how to optimize that platform, and then start spreading into the other spaces. You will catch those moments where you know you're ready to add another platform to the mix. You will start connecting authentically with people, but it's so important that you get yourself

## [06:02 - 06:14]

out there so that people know that you have something for them, that you are an expert in your niche, that you care and want to serve and that you have a story to tell. So pick a platform and start creating.
